title: Language Frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter in every <languages_dir>/<id>.md file
x-file-example:
---
id: python
collection: language
name: "Python"
execution: interpreted
package_managers:
  - pip
  - uv
  - poetry
  - conda
bundlers: null
versions:
  - "3.11"
  - "3.12"
  - "3.13"
tools:
  - "[[pytorch]]"
  - "[[fastapi]]"
concepts:
  - "[[dynamic_typing]]"
  - "[[garbage_collection]]"
tags:
  - scripting
  - data-science
---

## Overview
General-purpose interpreted language known for readability and a vast ecosystem.

## Strengths

## Weaknesses

## Notes

id.description: Snake_case ID used as the filename and in [[wikilinks]]

collection.description: Node type — used by Obsidian-style apps to filter nodes by collection

name.description: Display name of the language as it appears in official documentation

execution.description: How source code is turned into running instructions — interpreted (runtime eval), compiled (ahead-of-time native binary), bytecode (compiled to VM bytecode, e.g. JVM/CLR), or transpiled (compiled to another high-level language)

package_managers.description: Package managers available for this language, in order of prevalence

bundlers.description: Build/bundle tools used to package or distribute code written in this language (e.g. webpack, vite, rollup, esbuild). Null if the language has no bundling step.

versions.description: Notable or currently supported versions, most recent last

tools.description: [[wikilink]] references to tool nodes written in or primarily used with this language

concepts.description: [[wikilink]] references to curriculum concepts that explain how this language works
